Container.Name: opensandbox-server
Container.Overview: Trusted OpenSandbox lifecycle service for ai-tg-bot. It owns Docker-socket access and creates persistent per-user runner containers; the bot itself remains unprivileged.
Container.Requires: Create the custom Docker network ai-tg-bot-opensandbox first. Copy docker/opensandbox/config.example.toml to the Config File host path, set allowed_host_paths to the Shared Host Folder users subtree, and use the same API key in ai-tg-bot. Docker-socket access gives this service root-equivalent authority over the Unraid host.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required by the OpenSandbox Docker runtime. Never mount this into ai-tg-bot or a runner container.

Config.Name: Config File
Config.Description: OpenSandbox TOML configuration. Seed it from docker/opensandbox/config.example.toml before starting.

Config.Name: Shared Host Folder
Config.Description: Must be the identical absolute host path used by ai-tg-bot. If you change it, also change this target, OPEN_SANDBOX_SHARED_HOST_ROOT, and allowed_host_paths.

Config.Name: OpenSandbox API Key
Config.Description: Long random secret. Set the identical value as OPEN_SANDBOX_API_KEY in ai-tg-bot.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Path to the mounted server configuration.
